title: Introduction
summary: Overview of Hatchcom 5 software, system requirements, installation, and basic operations for managing hatchery equipment.

children.title: Help System
children.summary: Accessing and using the Hatchcom help documentation for troubleshooting and guidance.

children.title: Opening Help Files
children.summary: Multiple methods to access the Hatchcom help documentation.
children.content:
When solving problems, you may find it convenient to use the Hatchcom help file. There are several ways to open the help file.

Click the question mark help button on the main screen. Click the question mark icon on a dialog window to jump directly to the help section relevant to the current function. Press the F1 function key as an alternative means to choose context help.

children.title: Customer Support
children.summary: Contact information and support scope for Jamesway Hatchcom 5.

children.title: Support Contacts and Scope
children.summary: How to contact Jamesway support and what types of issues are covered.
children.content:
Jamesway provides customer support for Hatchcom 5. Contact your local agent or the office nearest you. Telephone number and email address are located on the inside title page.

Jamesway does not supply technical help for Windows problems, configuring local area networking, or for other software installed on your system. Please contact your IT department or the manufacturer of your software if problems should occur in these areas.

children.title: System Requirements
children.summary: Minimum hardware and software specifications for running Hatchcom 5.

children.title: PC Hardware Requirements
children.summary: Minimum computer specifications needed to run Hatchcom 5 efficiently.
children.content:
For Hatchcom 5 to run efficiently on your computer, the following minimum requirements must be met:

Desktop PC with Intel compatible CPU, 4 GB memory and 200 GB hard disk
Windows 7 Professional 64-bit edition or later
One USB port for security lock
Two additional USB ports if using fiber optic networking
One additional USB port if connecting to a Guardian HVAC control system
Two Ethernet ports if using Ethernet networking
Hatchcom software is supplied on USB flash drive

children.title: Hardware Installation
children.summary: Physical installation and connection of Hatchcom hardware components.

children.title: Connecting to Incubators and Hatchers
children.summary: Procedures for connecting Hatchcom to various types of incubator equipment.
children.content:
When you are connecting Hatchcom to incubators or hatchers using fiber optic or Belden cables, it is necessary to attach a PTA703 USB cable assembly and PTA706 converter box. The picture below indicates where to connect the cable assembly to converter box.

Platinum machines connected using Ethernet do not require the converter box. It is recommended that the Hatchcom PC uses a secondary Ethernet adapter to connect to the incubators and hatchers, minimizing network traffic on the office network.

The Hatchcom software must be installed before plugging USB devices into PC. This ensures that the required device drivers are available without the need of a manual driver install.

Install the Hatchcom security lock into USB port on rear side of computer when prompted to do so at the end of the Hatchcom installation. Either one of the two types of locks shown is compatible.

If you have a fiber converter box plug in the USB cables. If you plug in the cables after the Hatchcom installer exits, then it will be necessary to reboot the PC to allow the system to recognize the cables.
children.section: Introduction/Hardware Installation/Connecting to Incubators and Hatchers

children.title: Cable Types and Connections
children.summary: Different cable types and their connection requirements for various equipment.
children.content:
Refer to the Platinum, PT-100, or PS501 installation manuals for instructions on crimping and polishing the Ethernet, fiber optic, or Belden cables. Each unit's station address needs to be set as described in the manuals before the Hatchcom network can be configured.

If Hatchcom is used in a hatchery with single-stage ACI equipment, then the fiber optic cable must be connected to a Sentry display panel and the panel's Hatchcom feature must be enabled. In this case Hatchcom will not work correctly when the fiber optic cable is connected to a fiber optic hub. For best performance Hatchcom should be connected to master display panel.

children.title: Remote Client Connection
children.summary: Configuring remote access to Hatchcom from other computers or devices.

children.title: Remote Access Methods
children.summary: Two primary methods for accessing Hatchcom remotely.
children.content:
Hatchcom provides two different methods for remote access:

The Hatchcom client program can be installed on any Intel processor-based PC or tablet running the home or professional Windows version. The remote PC can access all Hatchcom functions when there is a direct network connection to the Hatchcom server PC. This could be a LAN (local access network) inside the hatchery, a Wi-Fi network inside the hatchery, or a VPN (virtual private network) connection from anywhere over the Internet. Note that the VPN connections can be difficult to set up because the hatchery network is typically protected through firewalls and user access restrictions.

Use a remote desktop control application such as Windows Remote Desktop or TeamViewer to allow a user to take control of the Hatchcom computer. This is easier to set up, especially if you desire to access more than one Hatchcom computer. The downside is that screen updates are a little slower and a user sitting in front of the main Hatchcom computer must stop working on the computer while someone else is using it remotely. This method will work for any device for which a compatible remote client app is available.

children.title: Remote Client Installation and Configuration
children.summary: Steps to install and configure the Hatchcom remote client software.
children.content:
Install the Hatchcom client using the standard Hatchcom Setup installer but choose the remote client type when prompted. The client PC must be connected to the hatchery's LAN or Wi-Fi network, not the segregated ethernet network used for Platinum incubators. When Hatchcom client runs the first time it will prompt to set the Hatchcom server PC's computer name if the DNS server is configured to translate the computer name to its address, or the IP address when it is set statically. Note that the DNS name may be different from the Windows PC name. Test the connection by issuing 'ping name' in a Windows command prompt.

For access from outside of hatchery TeamViewer is recommended because it is easier to configure through the hatchery firewall. If you prefer to remotely connect the Hatchcom client program over the Internet, it is necessary to use a VPN. Its setup depends on the type of Internet router or firewall appliance that is used at the hatchery and will require an experienced IT professional that is familiar with the hatchery's networking hardware to configure. When the VPN is correctly configured on the remote PC to reach the Hatchcom PC inside the hatchery, you will first establish the VPN connection then run the Hatchcom client software.
children.section: Introduction/Remote Client Connection/Remote Client Installation and Configuration

children.title: Troubleshooting Remote Connections
children.summary: Common issues and solutions for remote connection problems.
children.content: If the remote client fails to connect to the Hatchcom server PC, then security software or third-party firewalls on the client or server PC's may be preventing the connection. Cyber security software such as Bitdefender or Kaspersky Internet Security are known to block communication.

children.title: Value Entry
children.summary: Using the value entry dialog for inputting settings and passwords.

children.title: Value Entry Dialog Controls
children.summary: Functions of the various buttons in the value entry dialog.
children.content:
Many screens use a value entry dialog that ensures only acceptable settings can be entered.

Increases the entered value. If the value is outside the allowable range, then the largest possible value is shown and no further increases are made. You may also use the Up key to increase number.

Decreases the entered value. If the value is outside the allowable range, then the lowest possible value is shown and no further decreases are made. You may also use the Down key to decrease number.

Add a number to current value. The current value will be erased the first time a number button is pressed. You may also use the keyboard to type numbers.

C Clears the current value. You may also use the C key to clear the value.

Enter a decimal place. This button is shown only if the entry allows decimal numbers.

Accept the new value. This button is grayed out when the current value is outside the allowable range.

Cancel the value entry without making changes.

children.title: Password Entry
children.summary: Using the modified value entry dialog for password-protected functions.
children.content:
When you attempt to access a function that is password protected, you will be shown a modified value entry dialog. As you type the password, the numerals are hidden by the asterisk * symbol. Refer to passwords section for information on controlling user access.

Hatchcom does not indicate what level of access is required to proceed. Entering the level 2 password will always be accepted but entering the level 1 password will deny access with the following message if the function is programmed to require level 2.

title: Main Screen
summary: Navigation and configuration of the primary Hatchcom interface for monitoring and controlling hatchery equipment.

children.title: Main Screen Layout
children.summary: Overview of the main screen interface elements and their functions.

children.title: Screen Elements and Functions
children.summary: Description of all interface elements on the main Hatchcom screen.
children.content:
When you launch the Hatchcom program, the first screen to appear is the main screen showing the hatchery layout. This is your Home screen. Use this screen to manage the machines in the following ways:

See an overview of all machines and their critical sensor readings such as temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide actual readings and current alarms.
View each machine's status indicated by its background color.
Generate reports showing present or past activity of all machines.
Configure user preferences, alarm calling, and database settings.
Get detailed information on a machine by double-clicking the unit.
Quickly access commonly used functions by right-clicking the unit.
Change the hatchery layout and customize machine status colors or which sensor readings are displayed.
If a Guardian HVAC control system is enabled, the HVAC information and status for each Guardian-controlled room will also be displayed.

When launching Hatchcom for the first time after installation, the layout screen will be blank and you will be prompted whether you want to edit the layout. Click Yes and proceed to the edit hatchery layout section.

You may also import settings from an existing Hatchcom Ill installation. Use the Hatchcom 3 Export utility supplied in the miscellaneous folder on the USB flash drive at the old Hatchcom PC to extract the settings file. Transfer the settings file to the Hatchcom 5 PC and click Import button to read into the database. If you have a Hatchcom 4 system simply run the upgrade installer to update to Hatchcom 5, it is not necessary to import the settings.
children.section: Main Screen/Main Screen Layout/Screen Elements and Functions

children.title: Report Buttons and Functions
children.summary: Functions of the various report buttons on the main screen toolbar.
children.content:
Screen report displays all current setpoint and actual readings for each incubator, hatcher or Guardian-controlled room.

Alarm report displays a history of alarms for all machines, showing each alarm's start and end time and the sensor readings at the time the alarm commenced. To display a single unit report right-click the unit and choose alarm report in the context menu.

Statistic report displays averaged sensor readings over a period of time. It also shows the minimum and maximum extents and percentage of time that heating, cooling and other outputs were operating.

Scheduled maintenance report allows the user to set periodic maintenance tasks for incubators and HVAC units and keep track of unfinished tasks. To display a single unit report right-click the unit and choose maintenance in the context menu.

Scheduled maintenance report button with an indicator that tasks are due.

Configure alarm callout to automatically send an e-mail or initiate a call when an alarm occurs. You may specify which alarms will trigger callout and the time the day that the system will operate.
children.section: Main Screen/Main Screen Layout/Report Buttons and Functions

children.title: Configuration and Utility Buttons
children.summary: Functions of configuration and utility buttons on the main screen.
children.content:
Edit layout function allows you to configure incubators, hatchers, and Guardian-controlled rooms and arrange them to match the building layout. You may also customize the status colors and select the actual readings that are displayed.

Database function allows you to schedule automatic backups of the database, import or export database settings, and view archived databases.

Preference screen allows you to set display units for temperature, humidity, pressure and other settings. When connecting remotely use this screen to choose the main Hatchcom computer name.

Help function opens the user manual.

The group selection button is displayed whenever a unit is selected. It allows you to perform the same action on several units at the same time.

Each unit is shown as a rectangle with its current readings and any active alarms. The background color indicates the current machine status listed below in priority order from highest to lowest. The colors shown are the defaults used; they may be changed in the edit layout function. See alarm description section for list of alarm icons.
children.section: Main Screen/Main Screen Layout/Configuration and Utility Buttons

children.title: Main Screen Navigation
children.summary: Methods for navigating and interacting with the main screen interface.

children.title: Zooming and Scrolling
children.summary: Using zoom and scroll functions to navigate the hatchery layout.
children.content:
There are several ways to navigate the main screen. Click on the (+) and - buttons on the left edge of the screen to zoom in or out, or use the zoom all button to scale the entire layout to fit into the window. You may also use the scroll wheel on the mouse to zoom in or out.

When only part of the hatchery layout is visible, use the scrollbars to reposition the hatchery view or click and hold the left mouse button to drag the layout.

If special room objects have been defined in the layout, click the middle mouse button (scroll wheel) or double-click left button on a point not covered by any units to zoom in to show the entire room area. Click the middle mouse button again to return to previous zoom position.
children.section: Main Screen/Main Screen Navigation/Zooming and Scrolling

children.title: Unit Interaction Methods
children.summary: Methods for interacting with machine units on the main screen.
children.content:
Double-click one of the units with the left mouse button to show the unit's machine state screen. If the machine state screen for the selected unit is already open but hidden behind the main screen, then the window will be moved to the front.

Click the right mouse button on one of the units to display its context menu. The context menu contains frequently used functions to display reports and manipulate profiles. The functions that are listed in the menu depend on the type of machine and its current status.
children.section: Main Screen/Main Screen Navigation/Unit Interaction Methods

children.title: Context Menu Functions
children.summary: Functions available in the right-click context menu for machine units.
children.content:
The context menu shows the following functions:

Machine state screen shows all of the machine's setpoints, actual sensor readings, and machine status.
Alarm report displays a history of alarms for selected machine or room, showing each alarm's start and end time and the sensor readings at the time the alarm commenced.
Numeric report presents a detailed listing of temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide and other readings for selected machine or room over a period of time.
Setpoint report lists all setpoint changes made to a machine or room over a period of time. The setpoint changes could have been made by a profile or schedule, or by the user selecting fixed setpoints.
Profile Report lists all profile and setting changes made to a machine over a period of time. The changes could have been made by an operator from Hatchcom or locally at a Platinum touch screen.
Maintenance report allows the user to view current maintenance tasks that need to be performed on a specific machine or room. You may also complete a task or create a new one-time task in this screen.
Graph function allows you to see a graphic representation of past performance using the same data generated in the numeric report.
Change setpoints allows you to modify the fixed setpoints on a CM setter or hatcher.
View profile function shows the currently active profile, or you can view the stage program on CM machines. View screen has commands to save or print the profile. This button is only visible if the machine is running a profile.
Start profile allows you to run a profile or control stage program on an incubator or hatcher.
Edit profile allows you to modify a profile or edit stage program on an incubator or hatcher.
Edit Encore profile function is available on egg storage machines only. It can be used to create a new SPIDES profile or make changes to an existing profile.
Transfer profile start date is present on incubators if it is currently running a profile. This function allows the profile and profile start date to carry over to one to four hatchers that have received eggs from that incubator.
Alarm status screen lists all the currently active alarms, with additional commands to generate reports or activate alarm override.
Schedule function configures automatic setpoint changes at predefined days during the week on Guardian-controlled rooms.
Group selection function allows you to perform the same action on several units at the same time.
children.section: Main Screen/Main Screen Navigation/Context Menu Functions

children.title: Group Selection
children.summary: Selecting multiple machines and performing batch operations on them.

children.title: Group Selection Methods
children.summary: Different methods for selecting multiple machines for group operations.
children.content:
With the group selection function, you can select a list of machines to perform certain functions on each machine in one step. To start the group selection, pick one of the units in the layout and click the group selection button at the top of the screen or select it in the context menu after clicking the right mouse button. The main screen is now in group selection mode.

You may now select additional units of the same type by clicking once inside each rectangle. To remove one of the units from the selection click the unit a second time. You may also select a range of machines using rubber band by holding down the Shift key. If a single unit is selected, the Ctrl-A key combination will select all other units of the same type, else all units will be selected. Click a room object to select all machines that have same room assignment and type, else if the room does not have a number assigned select all machines that it intersects.

Once all the required units have been selected, click one of the command buttons at the top of the screen. Hatchcom will not allow selecting units that are of different type. For example, picking an incubator initially will disallow selecting Guardian-controlled rooms. Some command functions will become unavailable if both incubator and hatcher types are selected at the same time, as they are not compatible with both types of machines.

children.title: Group Command Functions
children.summary: Available functions when working with multiple selected machines.
children.content:
Cancel the group function command.

Alarm report displays a history of alarms for all selected machines.

Statistic report displays averaged sensor readings over a period of time for all selected machines.

Maintenance report shows current maintenance tasks that need to be performed on selected machines. You may also complete a task or create new one-time tasks that are linked only to the selected units.

Place all selected units online. Hatchcom will then attempt to communicate with these machines and update the status color accordingly.

Place all selected units offline. Hatchcom will stop accepting data from these units. This function should be used whenever units will be powered off for an extended period of time. The units' status color will change to offline (default is dark gray).

Setpoint allows you to modify the temperature, humidity, damper, or carbon dioxide setpoints in one operation, or on CM machines change fixed setpoints. Note: Active profiles will be cancelled without confirmation.

Start profile allows you to run a profile or control stage program on all selected incubators or hatchers.

Note: The profile does not start if selected profile slot is unused. After sending the command, verify on each unit that the intended profile is running.

On CM machines allows you to edit stage program on all selected setters or hatchers.

On CM machines allows you to change engineer settings on all selected setters or hatchers.

Schedule function allows you to configure automatic setpoint changes at predefined days during the week on Guardian-controlled rooms. After sending the command, verify on each unit that the intended schedule is running.

Silence alarms on all selected units.

children.title: Change Setpoints
children.summary: Modifying temperature, humidity, damper, and carbon dioxide setpoints for machines.

children.title: Setpoint Modification Interface
children.summary: Interface for changing setpoints on multiple selected machines.
children.content:
Use the setpoint function to modify the temperature, humidity, damper, or carbon dioxide setpoints in one operation on all selected machines. On multi-stage machines only temperature and humidity setpoints are available. On Platinum egg storage machines only temperature and damper are available. Select the override option to start the alarm override when the setpoints are sent to machine.

Press button to change fixed temperature setpoint. Current temperature setpoint is shown in units as set in the preference screen.

Press button to change fixed humidity setpoint. Current humidity setpoint is shown in units as set in the preference screen.

Press button to change fixed damp setpoint. Current setpoint is shown in percent open. There is no damper setpoint when carbon dioxide setpoint is selected.

Press button to change fixed carbon dioxide setpoint. Current setpoint is shown in units as set in the preference screen. There is no carbon dioxide setpoint when damper setpoint is selected.

Press the button to toggle alarm override on or off, it will be activated when button is colored orange. Use the alarm override function to set the alarms that are overridden.

Once desired setpoints have been entered, accept the new setpoints. Note: Active profiles will be cancelled without confirmation.

Cancel without making changes to the setpoints.

children.title: Edit Hatchery Layout
children.summary: Configuring and arranging incubators, hatchers, and HVAC rooms in the layout view.

children.title: Layout Editing Functions
children.summary: Functions available for editing the hatchery layout.
children.content:
Use the edit function to configure incubators, hatchers, and Guardian-controlled rooms as well as to arrange the units to match the building layout.

When launching Hatchcom for the first time after installation, you will be prompted to edit the hatchery layout. After selecting Yes all Ethernet connected incubators and hatchers will automatically appear in the layout. Other network types must be configured manually by clicking one of the configuration buttons.

Once all units have been configured, arrange the unit rectangles to match the hatchery building layout. You may use the arrange machines function to quickly position the units into several rows. When several machines are selected the function will arrange those units only.

Click on one of the rectangular unit shapes and move the unit while holding down the mouse button. Several units can be moved in a single operation by holding down the Ctrl key while selecting several units or by holding down the Shift key to drag the rubber band around the units to be selected. Release the key, then move selected units with mouse as described above.

To resize the rectangle, click on one of the resize handles and drag the mouse pointer. Hold down the Ctrl key to maintain aspect ratio. If several units are selected, then they will be resized likewise. If you make a mistake, use the undo function to restore the layout to a previous state.

The configuration functions will only add new units to the layout, but may prompt to remove units that are in the layout but did not respond to automatic configuration. Any units that should no longer appear in the layout must be manually selected and removed using the delete function. Reboot the Hatchcom PC to drop non-operational machine numbers.
children.section: Main Screen/Edit Hatchery Layout/Layout Editing Functions

children.title: Layout Toolbar Functions
children.summary: Functions available on the layout editing toolbar.
children.content:
The following functions are available while editing the layout:

Configure Ethernet or RS-485 connected CM setters and hatchers. Hatchcom can automatically scan network for Ethernet connected machines or user can manually add legacy systems.
Configure Guardian HVAC panels connected to RS-485 network. Hatchcom will try all possible PLC addresses and list units that respond, showing their installed options.
Display a menu to choose the colors associated with each machine status. When prompted for the color you may set the Alpha channel to 0 to remove the status from the priority order. You may also reset the status colors to factory defaults.
Choose which actual values are displayed on each unit. Selecting fewer readings to display will increase the font size of sensor readings in the layout view. Values are shown horizontally when the unit's width exceeds four times its height. You may also select whether Hatchcom only highlights audibly alarming units or have Hatchcom scroll the hatchery layout to make newly alarming machines visible.
Export 360 function generates the hatchery layout file for import into the 360 cloud platform.

The left side of the screen has the tool buttons to edit the layout:
Toggle the button to view sensor readings when in edit mode. If the readings are not visible after first configuration, save the layout and then return to edit mode. Ctrl-click the zoom buttons to change font scale or reset with zoom all button.
Undo the last change. Use Ctrl+Z key as a shortcut.
Edit the selected object's properties. See layout object properties section for detailed instructions. Use P key as a shortcut or double-click left mouse button on the object.
Delete the selected object. Use Delete key as a shortcut.
Rotate the selected object. Use R key as a shortcut.
Arrange selected machines into rows or columns. See arrange machines section for detailed instructions. If no machines are selected, then all units will be arranged.
Duplicate the selected object. Use D key as a shortcut. Machine objects cannot be duplicated.
Add a new shape object.
Add a new text object. Use T key as a shortcut.
Add a new room object to permit zooming with a middle mouse button click. When two or more machines are selected, the new room object will enclose them.
Add a new horizontal wall object. Use H key as a shortcut.
Add a new vertical wall object. Use V key as a shortcut.
Add or change the backdrop image. Hatchcom allows only one backdrop image.
Bring object in front of all other objects on same level. Use the object property function to change object's level value. Use F key as a shortcut.
Place object behind all other objects on same level. Use the object property function to change object's level value. Use B key as a shortcut.
children.section: Main Screen/Edit Hatchery Layout/Layout Toolbar Functions

children.title: Layout Object Properties
children.summary: Configuring properties of objects in the hatchery layout.

children.title: Object Property Settings
children.summary: Various properties that can be configured for layout objects.
children.content:
Use the object properties to change appearance of the rectangle, such as colors and background image settings. The contents of the dialog will depend on the type of object.

Each machine can be assigned a custom room number between 1 and 20. When the room is not set (-) then single-stage machines will retain the room number programmed at the controller and multi-stage machines will not show a room number. When supported by the Platinum machine software, the room number will also appear on the machine.

The Modbus ID only needs to be set when interfacing a SCADA program to Hatchcom. Each incubator and HVAC panel will need to be assigned a unique ID number. It is recommended to use the Auto ID function to assign a unique ID the first time Hatchcom is configured, and then edit individually as new machines are added to the network.

The machine model number is only required for the 360 cloud platform. When not connecting to the 360 platform the machine model number does not need to be entered. The machine model can be selected by picking the cabinet capacity from the first combo box, then selecting the controls model number from the second combo box. Either read the controls model from the nameplate inside the control panel or choose the appropriate item from the list based on machine voltage and other machine options.

The machine model selection is only shown if the unit is supported by the 360 platform. The model can only be selected once Hatchcom has successfully communicated with the machine (i.e. the machine object in the layout is not black).

Room objects and HVAC panels have an additional combo box setting to select the room type. This setting is only required for the 360 cloud platform. It can be left blank if room type assignment is not required.

Add a background image to the machine or shape. When image is present the machine status color will be drawn with some transparency to make sure image is still visible.
Remove background image from object.
Change the background color of the object.
For machines, this color is visible only if its current status is normal. If set to black, then the object will display the standard normal color in place of black. The background can be made translucent by setting the Alpha channel value between 0 and 255, where 0 is completely transparent and 255 is opaque.
When enabled, draws a frame around object.
When enabled, makes the object's background transparent.
Text to be displayed on object.
When entry is left empty, the short identification text will be shown for incubators and hatchers, and for Guardian-controlled rooms it will display the panel description. Use the @ placeholder to insert the machine's unit number or ^ to insert room. Optionally, the machine's unit text can be customized by appending a semicolon, followed by the custom unit text.
When enabled, centers the machine name in the object.
Change the text color of the object.
Set the object level. Objects with a higher number will be placed in front of objects with a lower number.
Set the room number. The room object's room number can be left blank if it is not associated with an incubator or hatcher room.
On multi-stage hatchers only, turn on this option to configure as PX hatcher type.
children.section: Main Screen/Layout Object Properties/Object Property Settings

children.title: Advanced Object Properties
children.summary: Advanced properties and functions for layout objects.
children.content:
Automatic Modbus ID function assigns each machine a unique ID value. Use this function with caution as it will renumber every unit in the layout.

Print a table of all machines showing the assigned Modbus ID.

Displays the model picker screen to select machine model by selecting its options. The machine model only needs to be selected when using the 360 cloud platform.

Set general shape objects to rectangle mode.
Set general shape objects to rounded rectangle mode.
Set general shape objects to oval mode.
Global function will apply changes to all other units of the same type when selected.
Accept the changes to the object.
Cancel properties dialog without making changes.

Default short machine identifiers used by Hatchcom:

Identifier Description
SCI Single-stage chicken incubator
SCH Single-stage chicken hatcher
STI Single-stage turkey incubator
STH Single-stage turkey hatcher
MSI Multi-stage incubator
MSH Multi-stage hatcher
ENC Encore egg storage machine
HVR Guardian HVAC room panel
HVP Guardian HVAC plenum panel
SCM setter
HCM hatcher
children.section: Main Screen/Layout Object Properties/Advanced Object Properties

children.title: Arrange Machines
children.summary: Automatically arranging machines in the layout in grid structures.

children.title: Machine Arrangement Functions
children.summary: Functions for automatically arranging machines in the layout.
children.content:
Use this function to automatically arrange units in a grid structure. You will be prompted to select the direction in which the units will be ordered. The number quantity chooses how many units are added to each row or column before the following units shift to the next row or column.

The first unit that is selected will determine the location where the lowest numbered unit is placed into the layout. Use one of these methods to make sure the correct starting location is chosen before clicking the arrange function:

Click the first unit with the mouse, then click additional units while holding down Ctrl key
Click the first unit with the mouse, then press Ctrl+A to select all other units of the same type, or press Ctrl+A a second time to select all machines in the layout
Hold down the Shift key and drag the mouse to select units, making sure the first unit becomes highlighted first

Arrange machines to the right with unit numbers increasing from left to right.
Arrange machines to the left with unit numbers increasing from right to left.
Arrange machines down with unit numbers increasing from top to bottom.
Arrange machines up with unit numbers increasing from bottom to top.
Select the number of units that will be placed in one row (left or right mode) or in one column (up or down mode) before following units shift to the next row or column.
Arrange selected machines.
Cancel without making changes.

children.title: Machine Configuration
children.summary: Configuring Hatchcom to recognize and communicate with various machine types.

children.title: Single-stage and Multi-stage Configuration
children.summary: Configuring single-stage and multi-stage incubators and hatchers.
children.content:
The configuration function allows Hatchcom to recognize single-stage or multi-stage incubators and hatchers connected via fiber optic or legacy Belden cable.

Note: This step is not required when single-stage machines are connected via Ethernet or when Hatchcom is connected to a Sentry display via fiber optics. In these cases, the list of machines will be detected automatically. If these machines are not shown in the layout then the Ethernet is not connected properly, or the Sentry display panel has not been configured correctly. Refer to the appropriate manuals for configuration procedure.

Configure the single-stage or multi-stage network. Hatchcom will try all possible address combinations and list units that respond.
Set the date and time on single-stage network to be same as the Hatchcom PC.
Manually add a machine to layout. This is an advanced command that should only be used to configure units that are not powered on. See manual configuration for more information.
Once configuration has completed, accept the configured machines. Each newly added machine will have a rectangle added to the layout to the right of all existing units.
Cancel the configuration without making changes to the layout.
children.section: Main Screen/Machine Configuration/Single-stage and Multi-stage Configuration

children.title: Manual Configuration
children.summary: Manually adding machines to the layout when automatic detection fails.

children.title: Manual Machine Addition
children.summary: Procedures for manually adding machines to the Hatchcom layout.
children.content:
Manually add a machine to this layout. This is an advanced command that should only be used to configure units that are not powered on. This function should not be used to add units that did not respond to automatic configuration or units that are connected on Ethernet. You must correct the physical network problem preventing the unit(s) from being detected.

Select the machine type from the list and set the desired unit number. If the machine supports room number then also set this value, otherwise this setting is hidden. When you accept the manual configuration, the unit will be added to the list in the configure screen.
Select the room number for machine. This value is displayed only if the machine supports rooms.
Select the unit number for the machine.
Add the machine to the configure list.
Cancel the dialog without making changes.

children.title: CM Machine Configuration
children.summary: Configuring CM setters and hatchers connected via Ethernet or RS-485 networks.

children.title: CM Configuration Procedures
children.summary: Procedures for configuring CM setters and hatchers in Hatchcom.
children.content:
The CM configuration function allows Hatchcom to recognize CM setters and hatchers connected via Ethernet or legacy RS-485 networks.

Hatchcom can automatically scan the Ethernet network for connected machines. Make sure each machine is assigned a unique communication ID in the display's Engineer 2 tab and that all machines are powered on.

Machines using the legacy RS-485 network must be entered manually one machine at a time, or a range can be added by holding down the Add Unit button for 3 seconds.

Settings will be highlighted red when there is a conflict. Examples of conflict:
Two machines have the same IP address
Two machines have the same serial adapter and communication ID
Two machines have the same unit number and machine type
Incompatible machine types are assigned to the same serial adapter
Invalid Ethernet IP address is specified

Manually add a machine to the configuration. Select communication port, communication ID, unit number, and machine type that uniquely identify this machine. Hold for 3 seconds to add several machines at once. You will be prompted for the number of machines.
Manually delete the selected machine from the configuration.
Scan the Ethernet network for CM machines having IP addresses in the 10.77.20.x or 10.77.21.x range. Hatchcom will automatically assign unit numbers based on the communication ID set on the machine.
Once configuration is complete, accept the configured machines. Each newly added machine will have a rectangle added to the layout to the right of all existing units.
Cancel the configuration without making changes to the layout.
children.section: Main Screen/CM Machine Configuration/CM Configuration Procedures

children.title: HVAC Configuration
children.summary: Configuring Hatchcom to communicate with Jamesway's Guardian HVAC control system.

children.title: HVAC Panel Configuration
children.summary: Procedures for configuring Guardian HVAC panels in Hatchcom.
children.content:
The HVAC feature allows Hatchcom to communicate with Jamesway's Guardian control system. Older HVAC panels are networked to Hatchcom using a RS-485 network that is separate from incubators and hatchers, or panels utilizing the Schneider PLC operate on the Platinum Ethernet network.

The configuration function allows Hatchcom to recognize all Guardian and Airway panels connected to it, and collect the configuration data from those panels, which includes the number of Guardian-controlled rooms or plenums on each panel. You can double check that each panel is configured with correct options and you can customize the panel descriptions.

Before the panels can be added to Hatchcom, you must select the correct communication port and baud rate. Click the Configure button to start the panel search on the RS-485 and Ethernet networks. When a serial communication port is selected, both serial and Ethernet are active. When the port is set to Ethernet then only this network will be active.

Ethernet panels in IP address range 10.77.19.101 to 10.77.19.149 are configured automatically. Panels can be manually added and removed by pressing the F10 and F11 keys respectively. When adding a panel, it must be given a unique ID in the range 101 to 149.

To temporarily suspend HVAC communication set the port to disabled.
When the panels have been configured, give each room or plenum a unique description. This will be the text used when the rooms and plenums are added to the hatchery layout. If you leave the description empty, Hatchcom will assign a generic type and unit number. Use the organizer function to re-order the panel list for screen and statistical reports.
Each newly added room or plenum will have a rectangle added to the layout to the right of all existing units. Hatchcom will not remove rooms or plenums that did not respond from the layout, you will need to select each of these panels and use the delete function to remove from the layout.

children.title: HVAC Configuration Interface
children.summary: Interface elements for configuring HVAC panels.
children.content:
Set panel description
Room index or IP address
Set panel order
Configure the Guardian panels using selected serial port and speed. Hatchcom will scan all possible PLC addresses and add panels that respond to the list. All Guardian panels must be powered on before configuring.
Once configuration has completed and description for each room or plenum is set, then accept the configuration.
Cancel the configuration without making changes to the layout.
Change the setpoint and calibration passwords for the Guardian touch screen that is selected in the list.
Toggle the panel organizer mode on or off. When organizer mode is on drag items or use the up and down arrows to move panels into desired order. When mode is turned off the panels will be renumbered in ascending order, then the configuration list will revert to normal Modbus ID order. The selected order will be used in the report, machine state, and graph screens to scroll through the unit list.
Move the selected panel up in the display order.
Move the selected panel down in the display order.

children.title: HVAC Configuration Icons
children.summary: Icons indicating Guardian panel configuration options.
children.content:
The configuration icons indicate how the Guardian panel has been programmed:

1 Room has hot water heating
2 Room has chilled water cooling
3 Room has a mixing box temperature sensor
4 Room has a humidity sensor
5 Room has a humidifier
6 Room has a pulse width modulating humidifier
7 Room has dehumidification enabled
8 Room has a pressure sensor
9 Room has analog pressure control
10 Room has floating point pressure control
11 Room has supply fan control enabled
12 First plenum control is enabled with pressure sensor
13 Second plenum control is enabled with pressure sensor
14 Airway panel controlling Modular ventilation system
15 Airway panel controlling CC3 climate control system
16 Heat recovery AHU 1 chilled water incubation return temperature
17 Heat recovery AHU 2 chilled water incubation return temperature
18 Heat recovery AHU 3 chilled water incubation return temperature
19 Heat recovery AHU 4 chilled water incubation return temperature
20 Water chiller 1 chilled water incubation supply temperature
21 Water chiller 2 chilled water incubation supply temperature
22 Water chiller 3 chilled water incubation supply temperature
23 Water chiller 4 chilled water incubation supply temperature
24 Hydronic system has hot water heating
25 Hydronic system has electric heating
26 Airway panel controlling air handler unit
27 Air handler has a heat recovery valve
28 Air handler has a chilled water valve
29 Air handler has a hot water valve
30 Air handler has a humidifier
31 Air handler has a dehumidifier
32 Air handler has an outdoor humidity sensor
33 Air handler has a combined humidity and temperature sensor

children.title: HVAC Configuration Details
children.summary: Detailed information about HVAC panel configuration settings.
children.content:
The Modbus ID represents the panel number of the Guardian controller, and Room Index number shows which of the four room positions the rooftop unit is connected to or the IP address when the Ethernet panel is added manually.

The Unit Number is the unique number Hatchcom assigns to each Guardian-controlled room or plenum and is based on the Guardian's Modbus ID and room.

The Panel Description is the name given to each Guardian-controlled room or plenum and will appear in all the machine selection screens. Typically, this will be the name of the room (i.e. Incubator Room 1). The user can customize this text by clicking once, then either select from the predefined list or type text up to 25 characters in length.

If the panel has a program error alarm, then the configuration reported by the Guardian panel does not match the configuration stored in the database. Select the panel in the list, Hatchcom will display a message indicating that the configurations differ and prompt the user whether to write Hatchcom's configuration back to PLC. If you know the Guardian panel's configuration is incorrect (for example after replacing the CPU module) answer Yes, otherwise answer No and run the configuration step so that Hatchcom records the correct configuration from PLC.

To view the touch screen passwords, select one of the panels in the list, and then click the Password button. Note that all panels with the same Modbus ID will have common passwords. To change a password, enter a new four digit number and press the Change button.

children.title: Export 360 Layout
children.summary: Generating layout files for import into the 360 cloud platform.

children.title: 360 Layout Export Requirements
children.summary: Requirements and prerequisites for exporting to the 360 cloud platform.
children.content:
This function generates the hatchery layout file which needs to be imported into the 360 cloud platform. It contains all necessary data that allows Hatchcom to interface with the 360 portal. Before generating the layout file, ensure the following items have been configured:

The edge server connection information is configured in the Preferences 360 tab.
The Sequential addressing mode is selected correctly in the Preferences General tab.
All incubators, hatchers, and HVAC panels have been added to the hatchery layout. Remove machines from the layout that should not be present.
The machine identifier shown at the bottom right corner of each machine is used to identify the machine in the 360 portal. Customize the text for each machine in the layout properties screen.
The room names will be transferred from the text field of each room object if they are configured, otherwise Hatchcom will assign generic room names. For example, to set the first room to be different than the default 'Setter Room 1', use this procedure:
1. Hold down Ctrl key and select the top-left and bottom-right machines to be in the room.
2. Click the Add Room toolbar button. The room will be dimensioned to outline the selected machines.
3. Click the Properties toolbar button to edit the room settings.
4. Set the desired room text, set the room number to 1, and set the room type to 'Setter' or 'Hatcher' as appropriate.
Each machine must have the correct model number selected in the properties screen. Hatchcom will show an error message if some machines are missing the model number.
The machines must have unique room and unit numbers. Hatchcom will show an error message if some machines have duplicate unit numbers. For example, if the layout contains a single-stage incubator and multi-stage incubator numbered 1 located in the same room, then one of the machines needs to be assigned to a different room or its unit number must be changed. The layout may have one incubator and one hatcher that have the same unit number and room number because the 360 platform separates them into different rooms.
Make all room number assignments before the layout is exported. The 360 platform will not map the machines correctly when the rooms are changed later. Whenever the room assignment or model numbers are changed the layout must be exported again to 360.
Optionally the layout may have a background image or company logo that will be transferred to the 360 platform. If there is no background image, then Hatchcom will try to use the image from a generic or text object if present.
children.section: Main Screen/Export 360 Layout/360 Layout Export Requirements

children.title: Export Methods and Options
children.summary: Different methods and options for exporting to the 360 platform.
children.content:
By default, all machines will be configured on 360. Select machines individually if not all machines should be used. After selecting the Export 360 function, you will be prompted to choose the rendering method. The Yes option should be selected for standard 360 installations.

Both PC's must connect to the same edge server so that they can be joined to one portal. This is an advanced feature that should only be used after consultation with Jamesway.
Export the layout by rendering all non-machine objects into the background image. This is the recommended method because it ensures that the 360 platform includes all images, wall objects and text items.
Export the layout including only the background image and machine objects. This option will not include wall objects or text items. Use this option when applying the merge option.
If you selected the merge option, Hatchcom will prompt for the layout json file generated by the second Hatchcom PC. Hatchcom will load the second layout and verify that there is no room number overlap, then prompt how the layouts are to be combined. The second layout can be scaled and moved above, below, or beside the first layout.
You will be prompted to select the filename for saving the layout. Log into the 360 platform, then import the file into the hatchery section.
children.section: Main Screen/Export 360 Layout/Export Methods and Options

children.title: Modbus Client Access
children.summary: Configuring Modbus TCP protocol access for SCADA applications.

children.title: Modbus Configuration
children.summary: Configuring Modbus access for SCADA applications.
children.content:
The Modbus feature allows industry standard SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) applications to access machine parameters using the Modbus TCP protocol. The Modbus client needs to connect to the Hatchcom PC over an Ethernet network using standard Modbus TCP port 502, using a single IP address acting as a gateway to the individual incubators and hatchers. Please consult the manual of your SCADA software to determine the procedure to access devices through a gateway. Hatchcom supports only one concurrent connection.

Use the layout object properties function to assign a unique device ID by which each machine can be identified. The valid device ID range is 1 to 247. After making any changes to the device ID configuration, it is vital to update the SCADA program's configuration accordingly.

Initially, the device ID of each machine will be blank, indicating that IDs have not yet been assigned. Ensure that all machines are present in the layout, then edit the properties of one of the machines and select the Auto ID button to assign a sequential device ID to each machine. The Auto ID function should only be used the first time when no IDs have been assigned. If this function is used later when a Modbus client is already in use, the device IDs may shift on all machines causing the client to show incorrect information until the client is reprogrammed with the new configuration.

When a new machine has been added to the hatchery network, its device ID will be blank. Select the machine and click the Edit Properties button to modify the Modbus ID. Use any ID that is not yet in use between 1 and 247. There will be an error message displayed if another machine already has the ID assigned to it.

children.title: Modbus Register Tables
children.summary: Modbus register addresses and their corresponding data values.
children.content:
Use the Print function to make a hardcopy printout of the device ID configuration for programming the Modbus client software. The bottom of the page shows a list of input registers that are accessible to the client program. Hatchcom does not offset the register addresses, make sure the client program accesses the register address as shown. Note that only a subset of registers may be available depending on the machine type.

The Modbus client program needs to use the Read Input Register (04) to query the Hatchcom server using addresses described in table below. Hatchcom will also respond to the Read Holding Register function (03) returning the same information. Do not use any offsets when selecting the register address.

Register Incubators and HVAC Airway air handler Units
1000 Machine unit number Machine unit number bit field (see notes)
1001 Temperature setpoint Supply temp setpoint 1/10 °F
1002 Temperature actual Supply temp actual 1/10 °F
1003 ^Humidity setpoint Humidity setpoint 1/10 °F or %RH
1004 ^Humidity actual Humidity actual 1/10 °F or %RH
1005 $Alarm relay status Alarm relay status > 0 if alarming
1006 *Egg temperature actual Outdoor temp actual 1/10 °F
1007 *Damper setpoint % open
1008 *Damper actual % open
1009 *Carbon dioxide setpoint ppm
1010 *Carbon dioxide actual ppm
1011 *Fan speed Supply fan actual % full speed
1012 *Profile day in cycle 1/100 day
1013 Alarm status LO Alarm status LO bit field (see below)
1014 Alarm status HI Alarm status HI bit field (see below)
1015 #Pressure setpoint Supply pressure setpoint 1/1000 inch w.c.
1016 #Pressure actual Supply pressure actual 1/1000 inch w.c.
1017 *Temp actual zone 2 Chilled water valve act 1/10 °F or % open
1018 *Temp actual zone 3 Hot water valve act 1/10 °F or % open
1019 *Temp actual zone 4 Heat recovery valve act 1/10 °F or % open
1020 *Turn count Outdoor humidity actual count or 1/10 %RH

Airway Modular and CC3 panel registers:

Register Hydronic primary Hydronic secondary Units
1000 Panel unit number Panel unit number bit field
1001 CHWIS temp actual CHWS temp actual 1/10 °F
1002 CHWIS temp setpoint CHWR temp actual 1/10 °F
1003 CHWIR temp actual HWS temp actual 1/10 °F
1004 CHWIR temp setpoint HWR temp actual 1/10 F
1005 Alarm status Alarm status >0 if alarming
1006 CHWRT temp actual 1/10 F
1007 CHWIS press actual Inch w.c.
1008 CHWIS press setpoint Inch w.c.
1009 CHWIR press actual Inch w.c.
1010 CHWIR press setpoint Inch w.c.
1011 HWIR press actual HWS press actual Inch w.c.
1012 HWIR press setpoint HWS press setpoint Inch w.c.
1013 Alarm status LO Alarm status LO bit field
1014 Alarm status HI Alarm status HI bit field
1015 CHWIS VFD A actual CHWRT VFD A actual % speed
1016 CHWIS VFD B actual CHWRT VFD B actual % speed
1017 HWIR VFD A actual % speed
1018 HWIR VFD B actual % speed
1019 CHWIR HR valve actual % open
1020 CHWIR HR valve setpoint % open
children.section: Main Screen/Modbus Client Access/Modbus Register Tables

children.title: Modbus Notes and Alarm Status
children.summary: Important notes and alarm status information for Modbus implementation.
children.content:
Notes:
1. Machine unit number is encoded as a bit field with format RRRTTTTUUUUUUUU where R represents the room number (0 = room 1, 7 = room 8), T is the machine type number (see table below), and U is the unit number.
2. Temperature and humidity values need to be divided by 10 to convert to final reading. For example, a register value of 990 is equivalent to 99.0 °F.
3. Humidity values marked ^ will be in units according to type of humidity sensor used by machine. On multi-stage machines, the sensor reads in degrees Fahrenheit wet bulb, while on single-stage machines and HVAC the sensor reads in percent relative humidity.
4. Profile day in cycle needs to be divided by 100 to convert to number of days. For example, a register value of 1800 is equivalent to day 18.00, where fractional day 0.01 is slightly less than 15 minutes.
5. Input registers marked * are active for single-stage incubators only depending on installed options.
6. The alarm relay status marked S is the only writeable register. Use the Modbus Write Register function (06) with a non-zero value to acknowledge the alarms.
7. The pressure registers marked # are active for Guardian rooms or plenums only. These two's-complement values can be positive or negative and need to be divided by 1000 to convert to units of inches water column.
8. Inactive registers will be zero. If Hatchcom has not received data from a machine for 10 minutes, its actual readings will be set to zero and alarm relay status will have a value of 2. The alarm status registers contain a bit field representation of active alarms (bit 1 is the least significant bit in the register):

Bit Alarm status LO Alarm status HI
1 High pressure Alarm bypass
2 Low pressure Damper sensor failure
3 High temperature Novram / clock error
4 High humidity Egg turn continuity
5 Low temperature Program error
6 Low humidity Carbon dioxide sensor
7 Dry wick / exhaust fan High carbon dioxide
8 Maintenance Egg temperature
9 Fan failure Pilot / HatchSense sensor
10 Door open
11 Damper failure
12 Communications
13 Egg turn failure
14 Alarm relay
15 Sensor failure
16 Sensor communications

Note that not all alarm types are implemented for every type of machine.

Machine type Description
8 Single-stage chicken incubator
9 Single-stage chicken hatcher
10 Single-stage turkey incubator
11 Single-stage turkey hatcher
14 Encore egg storage machine
16 Multi-stage incubator
17 Multi-stage hatcher
19 Guardian or Airway controlled panel
20 CM setter
21 CM hatcher
children.section: Main Screen/Modbus Client Access/Modbus Notes and Alarm Status

children.title: Database Configuration
children.summary: Managing database backups, imports, exports, and maintenance.

children.title: Database Management Functions
children.summary: Functions for managing the Hatchcom database.
children.content:
The database configuration screen allows you to perform several database related tasks:

- Define interval schedule for making complete database backup.
- Schedule an immediate database backup.
- Export all critical database settings to a text file which later can be restored.
- Load an archived database backup file for generation of reports and graphs.
- See date range of numeric data contained in the database or archived backup.

If you need to restore a previously backed up database file run the Hatchcom 5 installer program, then choose the Restore database file from backup when prompted to select the components to be installed. The installer also offers the option to Restore empty database file but choosing this option will result in the loss of all data including the hatchery layout. The layout and other information may then be imported from a settings backup file.

Turn on the Adjust report data to original time zone check box to display all times exactly as recorded by the Hatchcom server in reports and graphs, otherwise if an archive is viewed in a different time zone the dates and times will be adjusted accordingly.

children.title: Database Configuration Interface
children.summary: Interface elements for configuring database settings.
children.content:
Display the SQL Server configuration manager from Windows control panel. This may be used to configure database server to use a specific TCP port.
Check the database integrity. This operation may take several minutes to finish depending on the database size.
Display the database server's error log. Use this function when the database does not start.
When selected enables the automatic database backup function. The Hatchcom server will schedule the backup around midnight at the selected frequency. Backups will be stored in the configured location.
Select the number of days between full database backups.
Select the number of database backups to keep. When Hatchcom has reached this number of backups, it will delete the oldest file before creating the next backup.
Immediately instructs the Hatchcom server to create a backup file. Depending on database size this may take a while to complete. Hatchcom will pop up a message when backup is finished.
Choose the location where backups will be stored. If you change the location, then previous backups will be left in the previous location and do not count towards the number of backup files that are stored. Note: You must choose a location which SQL Server database engine running with limited user rights can access.
Export database settings to text file. Do not edit this file as it may fail to import later. Hatchcom will automatically export settings whenever hatchery layout changes are saved. Note: Only settings are saved, not sensor readings and related data.
Import database settings that were previously exported or automatically generated by Hatchcom when the hatchery layout was saved.
Open an archived database backup. You will be prompted for the filename, and a new Hatchcom instance will be launched. The backup restoration may take a while to process before the main screen opens. Note: Only a limited number of functions are available.
Exit the database configuration screen. All changes will be saved.

children.title: Alarm Callout Configuration
children.summary: Configuring automatic phone and email notifications for alarm conditions.

children.title: Alarm Callout Overview
children.summary: Overview of the alarm callout feature for automatic notifications.
children.content:
Alarm callout is a feature of Hatchcom which automatically places telephone calls or sends e-mails to a list of contacts when an alarm occurs.

You can specify:
Alarms which trigger Hatchcom to initiate a call.
Time period during the day when Hatchcom can call.
Delays before Hatchcom calls or when it calls again if problem persists.

If calling by phone, it is assumed that the modem is configured and hooked up to the computer and phone jack. Refer to Windows documentation for installing and setting up the modem. If only using the e-mail functionality then no modem needs to be connected to the PC.

The Hatchcom program uses the Windows TAPI interface; therefore the modem can be shared with other TAPI compliant programs. Note however that Hatchcom cannot call out while another program is using the modem, nor if a non TAPI compliant program is active and has exclusive control of the modem.

When using a standard data modem, if the modem calls out and reaches one of the persons on the phone list, that person will get a silent connection. It is up to the person being called to conclude that Hatchcom is trying to inform the person that an alarm has occurred at the hatchery.

Using the e-mail feature requires that the computer has a permanent internet connection to access a reliable e-mail server. Once Hatchcom has forwarded the e-mail message to the mail server, it has no control over the time required to send the e-mails to their destination. It is up to the user to ensure that a reliable e-mail server is used, and all message recipients have configured their inbox to accept e-mail from the server. Some cell phone providers allow e-mails to be sent directly to the phone as text messages, which may be used to provide detailed alarm data to the contact.

When sending an e-mail, the machine with the highest priority alarm that triggered the callout will be listed first, followed by all other machines that are also alarming. Note that only the highest priority alarm will be listed for each machine, therefore each machine may have additional alarms active at the same time. Machines will be shown with their short machine identifier.

The alarm callout screen shows five property pages. The first page is for general callout settings, the second page sets days and times callout is active, the third lists the contact information, the fourth selects which alarms will generate a callout, and the last page configures the optional e-mail server settings.
children.section: Main Screen/Alarm Callout Configuration/Alarm Callout Overview

children.title: Preferences
children.summary: Configuring user preferences, units, languages, and other settings.

children.title: Units Configuration
children.summary: Configuring measurement units for temperature, humidity, and other readings.
children.content:
The preferences dialog allows you to select the units used to display sensor readings such as temperature, humidity, or carbon dioxide and other user settings. The preference screen is divided into several tab pages.

Use this page to select units of measure used in Hatchcom.

Set temperature units to degrees Fahrenheit or degrees Celsius.
Set humidity units to percent relative humidity, degrees wet bulb, or use the sensor's units (relative for single-stage machines or wet bulb for multi-stage machines).
Set carbon dioxide units to percent or parts per million.
Set pressure units to metric such as Pascal and bar, or imperial such as inches of water column and pounds per square inch.
Set the decimal place mode to Windows system units, or display all decimal readings using a period or comma. Select the system decimal option to save reports using Unicode text encoding.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.

children.title: Date Format Configuration
children.summary: Configuring date and time display formats in Hatchcom.
children.content:
Use this page to select how dates are formatted in Hatchcom.

Select profile day in cycle to be displayed as:
2:12h fractional days
60h days and hours
12/31/99

Select calendar date order to be displayed as:
31/12/99 month/day/year
day/month/year
99/12/31 or year/month/day

Select calendar date order to be displayed using Windows system date order.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.

children.title: General Preferences
children.summary: General preference settings including language and interface options.
children.content:
Use this page to select various preference options.

Select language to display in Hatchcom. A dialog will list all languages that are installed. Select the desired language or choose the blank option to hide text on all function buttons.
Sequential addressing mode allows newer Platinum machines to use a continuous address numbering system. Hatchcom will remove room numbers unless a room assignment was made in layout properties. Consult the Platinum user manual for the procedure on setting the address switches to work with sequential addressing mode.
Embedded main screen option allows Hatchcom to show an embedded state screen in the layout when sufficiently zoomed in. Note that this option greatly increases the memory usage of the Hatchcom program.
Change the password configuration.
Select the light user interface mode. The Hatchcom client will restart when mode is changed.
Select the dark user interface mode. The Hatchcom client will restart when mode is changed.
On Windows systems that support dark mode, adapts Hatchcom to use the Windows default app mode. Hatchcom will use light user interface mode if Windows does not support the feature.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.

The Do not use native file selection dialogs option only needs to be enabled in rare instances where a faulty Windows Explorer shell extension interferes with Hatchcom operation when selecting file names, for example when saving a report.
The Use classic print dialog option uses the legacy print dialog on Windows 11 where the embedded preview function is not available in Hatchcom. Note that this option will affect all Windows applications. Select the square option to restore the Windows default mode.
The Sound alarms on computer option can be enabled to play a sound on the local PC whenever there is an audible incubator alarm.

children.title: Language Selection
children.summary: Selecting display language for the Hatchcom interface.
children.content:
Use the language setting to select the language to be used for text items in the Hatchcom program. The dialog will list all the languages currently installed. You may also select a language neutral option that suppresses most of the English text, allowing navigation by icons.

Select the desired language as indicated by the name underneath the icon. The button with a blank name will select language neutral setting which removes all English text from command buttons.
Accept the new language setting.
Cancel language dialog without making changes.

children.title: Password Configuration
children.summary: Configuring password security for Hatchcom functions.
children.content:
Hatchcom provides password security access to functions that modify how the incubators and hatchers operate. Functions such as reports and graphs are not password protected since they do not affect machine operation.

When Hatchcom is connected to a Platinum or Sentry Network, Hatchcom will use the same passwords.

Two levels of access are provided:
- Use Level 1 Password (lower) for hatchery personnel who can change a few of the machine parameters.
- Use Level 2 Password (higher) to give your hatchery manager access to all machine functions.

Each of the two levels is programmed with its own four-digit numeric password.

Edit password level 1 value. When prompted enter password value between 1 and 9999. To disable the password press C to clear the value.
Edit password level 2 value. When prompted enter password value between 1 and 9999. To disable the password press C to clear the value.
No Password - The selected function does not require a password to enter.
Level 1 - Use a level 1 password (lower) for hatchery personnel who can change a few of the machine's parameters. Any function that requires a level 1 password can also be accessed using the level 2 password.
Level 2 - Use level 2 password (higher) to give your hatchery manager access to all machine functions but restrict access to all other employees.
Level 2 Master - The function requires the level 2 password and can only be entered at the main Hatchcom console or local Platinum display.
Master display - The function does not require a password, but users can only enter the function on the main Hatchcom console, local Platinum display, or master Sentry display.
When selected, Hatchcom will prompt for password every time a function is accessed that requires a password.
When selected Hatchcom will prompt for password one time only the first time a protected function is accessed. You will not be asked again until program is closed.
When selected Hatchcom will never prompt for password. Note: The user will still be prompted for a password at Platinum touch screens and Sentry display panels.
When selected Hatchcom uses Windows logon name to authenticate users. When selecting this option, you will be prompted to set the password users and assign the level for each user. Unlisted users will be prompted to enter the password, but if the special * name is added then this level is used instead without prompting for password.
Accept the new password settings.
Cancel password dialog without making changes.

children.title: Remote Connection Configuration
children.summary: Configuring remote client connections to Hatchcom.
children.content:
Use this page to select remote connection options.

Remote mode enables the Hatchcom client to connect to the networked server on a different computer. Remote connection may fail if firewalls are blocking access.
When remote mode is turned on, choose the server computer name from a list of local PCs. Alternatively, you may enter the computer name or IP address directly into the entry field.
Use an optional security phrase to authenticate remote clients. When the phrase is set at the main Hatchcom PC, enter the matching phrase at each remote client else the connection is rejected.
Display the Hatchcom software version information. See information section for details.
Accept the new preference settings.
Cancel preference dialog without making changes.

title: Reports
summary: Generating and analyzing various reports for monitoring hatchery equipment performance and history.

children.title: Report Navigation
children.summary: Common interface elements and navigation for all report types.

children.title: Report Interface Overview
children.summary: Overview of common elements in all Hatchcom reports.
children.content:
Hatchcom provides several different types of reports that allow you to obtain detailed information about the current execution of the machines or past machine activity. The following reports are available:

- Screen report lists the current setpoints and actual readings of all machines.
- Alarm report lists past alarm activity showing date and time when alarm started and ended, along with sensor readings at the time alarm commenced.
- Statistic report displays averaged sensor readings over a time period, along with the minimum and maximum readings and activity percentage of heating, cooling and other outputs.
- Numeric report presents a detailed listing of temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide and other readings for selected machine over a time period.
- Setpoint report lists all setpoint changes made by a machine over a time period.
- Profile report lists all profile and setting changes made by operator over a time period.
- Scheduled maintenance report allows you to set periodic maintenance tasks for incubators and HVAC units and keep track of tasks that have been completed.

All reports have a similar screen layout with many options to select the type of machines and data columns to be included.

The statistic report screenshot below shows the common report features. Note that not all reports have the same feature set and some items are displayed differently.

- Room and machine type selections are only available in global reports selected from the ribbon of the Home screen.
- Data columns may be turned on or off from the Columns menu in some reports or have dedicated selection buttons instead of the menu.
- Reports displaying historical data have start and end controls to select the date range. All dates will be formatted as selected by date selection in preference screen.
- The alarm, numeric and setpoint reports present the data in a text format that can be selected and copied to another program such as Microsoft Excel.
The screen, profile and statistic report have context menus to obtain detailed information on selected unit.

children.title: Common Report Controls
children.summary: Common control buttons and functions available in all reports.
children.content:
Save report to disk file in tab delimited format or comma separated values format. The data can then be imported into other programs such as Microsoft Excel.
Preview the report for printing. You will be able to change options before printing the report to any Windows compatible printer device.
Print the report to any Windows compatible printer device.
Open a menu to select the data columns to be included in the report. Click on each menu item to turn on and off. The menu item will have a blue background when selected, or white when not selected.
Hide all columns that do not contain any data.
Show all columns including ones that are presently turned off.
On reports that display historical data set the start date and time. If this button cannot be pressed, change the date or time entry field beside it.
On reports that display historical data set the end date and time. If this button cannot be pressed, change the date or time entry field beside it.
On reports that display historical data, set the date range to cover the last 24 hours. The report will refresh immediately when pressed.
Refresh button appears when the user changes a date or time setting. Click the refresh button to reload the report data using selected date range.
Show single-stage incubators in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show single-stage hatchers in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show Platinum egg storage machines in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show multi-stage incubators in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show multi-stage hatchers in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show CM setters in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show CM hatchers in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show Guardian HVAC panels in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show Sentry display panels in report when selected. Hold button down to clear or select all machine types.
Show units assigned to room when selected. Any units that do not use room will be shown when room 1 is selected. Hold button down to clear or select all rooms.
Load report for previous machine.
Load report for next machine.
In the screen and statistic reports, click the right mouse button on a unit to show a context menu with additional functions. See context menu in the main screen for more information. In numeric, alarm, and setpoint reports the right mouse button will allow you to copy selected text and paste into another program such as Microsoft Excel.

children.title: Screen Report
children.summary: Displaying current setpoints and actual readings for all machines.

children.title: Screen Report Overview
children.summary: Overview of the screen report and its displayed information.
children.content:
